key_insight: ATLAS learns a diffusion-based sampler that directly generates Boltzmann-distributed amorphous structures from an energy function, enabling efficient sampling, steering, and design in rugged glassy landscapes.
engineering_relevance: It offers a scalable route to predict thermodynamics and optimize composition/structure in amorphous materials, reducing costly molecular simulation and inverse-design workloads by orders of magnitude.
method: Equivariant graph neural network parameterizes a time-reversible diffusion process.
Amortized across system size, temperature, and composition to sample and estimate free energies/entropies.
Can be steered toward target observables and coupled with an LLM agent for multi-objective materials search.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that ATLAS matches parallel tempering MCMC on 2D Kob-Andersen systems with below 0.2% free-energy error in the low-temperature glass regime using over 500-fold fewer energy evaluations, and it reproduces experimental short-range-order trends in metallic glasses.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and cost of the target energy model, so practical deployment may be limited when oracle evaluations or universal interatomic potentials are expensive or uncertain.
paper_title: ATLAS: A Foundation Neural Sampler for Amorphous Materials
